channel.title: Deploy a Trustgrid Node in AWS on
channel.description: Recent content in Deploy a Trustgrid Node in AWS on

item.title: AWS HA Wireguard Cluster
item.description: This tutorial covers fronting a clustered Trustgrid node in AWS with a Network Load Balancer (NLB) so that Wireguard clients connect to a single stable endpoint and the NLB directs traffic to the active cluster member. Only the active member of a Trustgrid cluster responds healthy to the load balancer health check, so the NLB always forwards traffic to the active node. Prerequisites A two-member Trustgrid HA cluster deployed in AWS.

item.title: AWS Cluster IP Failover
item.description: AWS Cluster IP failover requires the June 2026 release or later. On earlier releases the AWS cluster IP was assigned as an Elastic IP. This tutorial covers the cluster IP failover mechanism for AWS-hosted Trustgrid clusters. The active cluster member claims a configured IP as a secondary private IP on its data ENI via ec2:AssignPrivateIpAddresses (with AllowReassignment=true). On failover, the standby promotes itself and reclaims the same secondary IP on its own ENI — no AWS route-table updates required.

item.title: AWS Cluster Route Failover
item.description: This tutorial covers AWS route failover for a clustered Trustgrid deployment. On failover, the active member updates AWS route-table entries (ec2:CreateRoute / ec2:DeleteRoute) so that overlay CIDRs always point at the active member’s data ENI — no floating IP required. For the IP-based failover alternative, see AWS Cluster IP Failover. How it works Graceful Failover The Trustgrid appliance relinquishing the active role removes its AWS route-table entries. The appliance gaining the active role creates new route-table entries pointing at its own ENI.

item.title: Configure HA L4 Cluster in AWS
item.description: In a high-availability (HA) cluster, the cluster IP gives you a stable address that survives member failover. L4 connectors and services can both leverage it, depending on which side of the traffic flow the cluster sits on. Use one or both — they are independent. Connectors give clients a stable destination IP:port to connect to. Services give backends a stable source IP for connections originated from the cluster. Prerequisites A two-member Trustgrid HA cluster deployed in AWS.
